titleStmt.title: Letter from Mary Anne Curley, 14 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Mary Anne Curley

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Mrs. Mary Anne Curley to the Chief Secretaries Office. In the letter Mrs. Curley raises concerns over her four children who have been taken from her and her husband, who is serving at the front. Mrs. Curley mentions that she is receiving separation allowance and can provide a clean home for the children.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3730

correspAction.placeName: No. 3 Caseys Cottages, Little Bray, County Wicklow, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
N3 Caseys Cottages Little Bray To His Excellency My Lord Pardon
my acquainting you about my
four children which has been
taken and sent from me. I
have not even the satisfaction
of knowing their whereabouts.
My Husband is at present
at the Front Wishes me to
aquaint your Lordship on
the matter. I am receiving â
separation allowance and is
quite willing to support and
keep our children if they get
their Liberty I have a good
clean house for them I ack.
II Dr Kieran
for report
15/2/16
My Lord,
trusting you will
look into the matter
that they may get their
liberty. My Lord
I am Your most Obedient Mary Anne Curley N3 Caseys Cottages Little Bray
